title: What are regulated charges on my electricity bill?
text: Regulated charges are mandatory fees that appear on every electricity bill regardless of your supplier or tariff plan. They are set by the national energy regulator (RAE in Greece) and are entirely outside your supplier's control. The main regulated charges are: (1) Network Use-of-System (UoS) charges — fees for using the electricity distribution and transmission network infrastructure. These cover the cost of maintaining power lines, transformers, and substations. (2) Renewable Energy Levy (ETMEAR) — a contribution toward government-mandated subsidies for renewable energy sources such as solar and wind. This levy helps fund the green transition of the energy system. (3) Capacity Mechanism Charge — supports backup generation capacity to ensure grid reliability and prevent blackouts during peak demand periods. (4) Public Service Obligation (PSO) levy — covers social and environmental policy obligations such as subsidised rates for vulnerable households. All regulated charges are reviewed and updated annually by the regulator. They are billed as a separate line item on your invoice and are the same for all customers on the same network zone. If you believe a regulated charge has been applied incorrectly, you should contact customer service with your bill number and account number.
